introduction:
In Tamil Nadu, the Community Certificate (Caste Certificate) is issued by the Revenue Divisional Officer (RDO) / Tahsildar to certify SC, ST, MBC (Most Backward Class), BC, BCM (Backward Class Muslim), and DNC (Denotified Communities) status.

It is a critical document for reservations in government schools, colleges, public employment, and welfare schemes. Applications can be made online through the TN e-Sevai Portal or offline at the nearest e-Sevai centre or Tahsildar office.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Issued by the Tahsildar of the applicant's native taluk
  • Processing time: 15 working days (under TN RTS Act)
  • Fee: ₹60 (via e-Sevai)
  • Community certificate is permanent for SC/ST; BC/MBC renewed as required
  • Online service: TN e-Sevai Portal
  • Aadhaar-based verification is mandatory for e-Sevai applications
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • Ration Card
  • Passport
Community / Caste Proof
  • Father's Community Certificate
  • School Transfer Certificate (TC) with community
  • Patta / Chitta (land records of native village)
  • Birth Certificate mentioning community
Additional
  • Nativity Certificate from native village Panchayat / VAO
  • Income Certificate (for BC Non-Creamy Layer)
  • Passport-size photographs (2)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via TN e-Sevai Portal

Apply at TN e-Sevai Portal or visit the nearest e-Sevai centre.

  1. Visit Portal
    Go to tnesevai.tn.gov.in or the TN e-District Portal.
  2. Login / Register
    Register using your mobile number and verify via OTP.
  3. Select Service
    Navigate to Revenue Services → Community Certificate.
  4. Fill Application
    Enter name, DOB, community, father's name, native district, taluk, and village details.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload all required documents as PDF/JPG files.
  6. Pay Fee
    Pay ₹60 online via net banking / debit card / UPI.
  7. Submit & Track
    Submit and track using your reference number on the portal.
  8. Download Certificate
    Download the digitally signed Community Certificate once approved by the Tahsildar.
You may also apply via Common Service Centres (CSC) or Tamil Nadu Arasu Kabil offices.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at e-Sevai Centre / Tahsildar Office

  1. Visit e-Sevai Centre or Tahsildar Office
    Go to the nearest e-Sevai centre or the Tahsildar office of your native taluk.
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Obtain the Community Certificate application form and fill all required fields.
  3. Submit with Documents
    Submit the filled form with self-attested document copies and pay ₹60 at the counter.
  4. VAO / Village Officer Enquiry
    The Village Administrative Officer (VAO) will verify your native village records and certify caste details.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect your Community Certificate from the Tahsildar office within 15 working days.
Processing Time: 15 working days under TN Right to Services Act.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application

Track at TN e-Sevai Portal using your reference number.

Use for Scholarships

Use the Community Certificate to apply for TN Government Welfare Schemes and Central scholarships via NSP.

Grievance

Call 1800-425-1188 (TN e-Sevai helpline) or visit TN Chief Minister's Cell.
